Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-01-01/Complin.firstExpected: Extra Chorum, quando ab uno tantum recitatur Officium dicitur: Jube, Dómine, benedícere; et subjungitur congruens Benedictio.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-01-01/Complin.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:168 — the corpus rubric carries guillemets around `Jube, Dómine, benedícere;`. Perl strips them while the compositor preserves them; both are surface renderings of the same rubric sentence, not a liturgical-content disagreement.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-01-01/Complin.notes: Representative entry for the Divino Afflatu single-reciter Compline rubric. Fan out to all matching DA Compline rows with the same first-divergence text.

The compositor preserves that source-backed rubric prose; the legacy Perl compare surface drops it.

Reduced - 1955/2024-01-01/Compline/5dc10.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Prayers.txt:52 — the source encodes the cross as `+++`. Reduced - 1955/2024-01-01/Matins/29ec2a3.firstExpected: Pater Noster dicitur secreto usque ad Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem:
Reduced - 1955/2024-01-01/Matins/29ec2a3.firstActual: « Pater Noster » dicitur secreto usque ad « Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem: »
Reduced - 1955/2024-01-01/Matins/29ec2a3.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:1-2 — the corpus rubric carries guillemets around both `Pater Noster` and `Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem:`. Perl strips them while the compositor preserves them; both are surface renderings of the same rubric sentence, not a liturgical-content disagreement.
Reduced - 1955/2024-01-01/Matins/29ec2a3.notes: Representative entry for the Roman Matins pre-lesson guillemet family under Reduced 1955. Fan out to all 1955 Matins rows with the same first-divergence text.

Reduced - 1955/2024-01-06/Compline/5dc10.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Prayers.txt:52 — the source encodes the cross as `+++`. Perl renders that token as `✙︎`; the compositor currently normalizes it to `+`. This is a surface glyph-choice difference, not a text-selection error.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.firstExpected: Examen conscientiæ vel Pater Noster totum secreto.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.firstActual: Examen conscientiæ vel «Pater Noster» totum secreto.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:129 — the corpus source carries `«Pater Noster»` with guillemets. Both Perl (strip) and compositor (preserve) are defensible renderings of the same source token; neither is liturgically wrong. Phase 3 §3h defers to the corpus author's formatting.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.notes: Pattern affects every Compline row across every policy and date. This single entry is representative; the full row-key fan-out is a follow-up bulk-populate task (see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md).

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.firstActual: Ant. Allelúja, * Dóminus regnávit, decórem índuit, allelúja, allelúja.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.citation: Phase 3 plan §3h — the compositor does not emit `Psalmus N [M]` heading lines before each psalm in psalmody sections. Perl emits one per psalm at every Hour with psalmody. The fix lives in `packages/compositor/src/emit/sections.ts` on the psalmody-slot emission path and is deferred within §3h as a structural change; see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md for the pattern catalogue.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.notes: Representative entry for the missing-psalm-heading pattern. Affects Lauds, Prime, Terce, Sext, None, Vespers, Compline on nearly every date across every policy. Bulk-populate once the fix lands and remaining rows are re-classified.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/None/318c.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:4-8 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Doxologies.txt:1-20 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:664-672 and docs/phase-2-rubrical-engine-design.md:1469 — the Roman January offices set `Doxology=Epi` or a local `[Doxology]`, and fallback hymn resolution is supposed to substitute the seasonal stanza from `Psalterium/Doxologies` in place of the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close. The compositor follows that source-backed rule; the Perl comparison surface retains the unsubstituted fallback stanza.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/None/318c.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 January Terce/Sext/None fallback-hymn doxology family where the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close is replaced by the Christmas or Epiphany stanza required by the winning office.
